qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unvalidated disposition parameter in ActiveStorage proxy controllers enables HTTP header injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in DiskService#path_for where blob keys containing '../' allow arbitrary file system access. This finding describes HTTP header injection via unvalidated disposition parameters in proxy controllers reaching ContentDisposition.format. The root causes are different (missing filesystem path validation vs missing disposition parameter validation), the vulnerable code is different (DiskService#path_for vs ProxyController/show via streaming.rb and ContentDisposition.format), and the vulnerability classes are different (CWE-22 path traversal vs CRLF/HTTP header injection). Both affect ActiveStorage but target entirely separate code paths and security boundaries.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in ActiveStorage DiskService#path_for where blob keys with '../' sequences allow arbitrary file read/write/delete on the server (CWE-22). The only qualifying finding identifies HTTP header injection via unvalidated disposition parameters in ActiveStorage proxy controllers, which is a completely different root cause, vulnerable code path, and vulnerability class. No PoC execution is warranted.
evidence: Advisory root cause: DiskService#path_for does not validate that resolved filesystem path remains within storage root directory
Finding root cause: Proxy controllers pass user-controlled disposition parameter to ContentDisposition.format without validation
Finding vulnerable code: activestorage/app/controllers/active_storage/blobs/proxy_controller.rb, streaming.rb, actionpack ContentDisposition.format
Advisory vulnerability class: CWE-22 Path Traversal
Finding vulnerability class: HTTP Header Injection (CRLF injection)
